title: Google introduces Gemma 4 12B for local multimodal agents
friendlyTitle: Gemma 4 12B brings multimodal AI closer to laptops
summary: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a mid-sized multimodal model designed to run on laptops with around 16GB of memory. The model uses a unified, encoder-free architecture for vision and native audio inputs and is released under Apache 2.0.
friendlySummary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, a model built to handle text, images, and audio on everyday hardware. It is aimed at developers who want local multimodal AI without always relying on cloud services.
whyItMatters: Capable local multimodal models reduce dependence on cloud-only inference for some workflows. Developers and privacy-conscious teams can experiment with agents that process text, images, and audio closer to the device.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Local AI can be cheaper, faster, and more private for some tasks. This is useful for prototypes, offline workflows, and teams that need tighter control over data.
